commands.review_summary:
The branch pins Windows node-host allowlist shell execution to the resolved analyzed executable path, denies unresolved plans, adds Windows shadow-executable regression coverage, and includes that test in `test:windows:ci`.

PR surface: Source +8, Tests +173, Config 0. Total +181 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible with high confidence: current main launches the parsed Windows segment argv after allowlist approval, and the PR's native Windows proof demonstrates the bare-executable shadowing condition before binding to the trusted resolved path.

target.title: fix(exec): bind Windows allowlist execution path
target.body:
## Summary

### What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where Windows node-host users relying on exec allowlists could have an approved PowerShell payload execute through an ambiguous executable name instead of the executable identity established during policy analysis.

## Changes

### Why This Change Was Made

The Windows shell execution path now uses the existing analyzed execution plan and fails closed when that plan does not contain a resolved executable. This keeps policy evaluation and process launch bound to the same executable without changing the public config or protocol contract.

- Pin the final Windows shell argv to the resolved executable path.
- Deny execution when the resolved plan is unavailable or blocked.
- Add regression coverage for resolved-path pinning and fail-closed behavior.

## Validation

### Evidence

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/node-host/invoke-system-run.test.ts src/node-host/invoke-system-run-allowlist.test.ts src/infra/exec-approvals-analysis.test.ts` — 57 tests passed.
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/node-host/invoke-system-run-allowlist.ts src/node-host/invoke-system-run-allowlist.test.ts` — passed.
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/node-host/invoke-system-run-allowlist.ts src/node-host/invoke-system-run-allowlist.test.ts` — passed.
- `git diff --check` — passed.
- Fresh structured autoreview — no accepted/actionable findings; patch judged correct.

## Notes

### User Impact

Windows allowlist users can expect node-host execution to launch the same resolved executable that satisfied policy. Commands without an unambiguous resolved execution plan are denied.

Native Windows validation is left to the repository's Windows-scoped PR CI because the local remote-runner client was unavailable.

AI-assisted: yes.
